question:
Problem A-1
Let \( A \) be any set of 20 distinct integers chosen from the arithmetic progression \( 1,4,7, \ldots, 100 \). Prove that there must be two distinct integers in \( A \) whose sum is 104.
solution:
A-1.
Each of the twenty integers of \( A \) must be in one of the eighteen disjoint sets
\[
\{1\},\{52\},\{4,100\},\{7,97\},\{10,94\}, \ldots,\{49,55\} .
\]

Hence some (at least two) of the pairs \( \{4,100\}, \ldots,\{49,55\} \) must have two integers from \( A \). But the sum for each of these pairs is 104 .

kernel_variant.question:
Let $A$ be any set of $11$ distinct integers chosen from the arithmetic progression
\[5,\;11,\;17,\;\dots ,\;113.\]
Prove that there must be two distinct integers in $A$ whose sum is $124$.
kernel_variant.solution:
Denote by S=124.  Partition the terms of the given progression into disjoint subsets as follows:

{5},    {11,113}, {17,107}, {23,101}, {29,95}, {35,89}, {41,83}, {47,77}, {53,71}, {59,65}.

Each two-element set {x, S-x} consists of members of the progression whose sum is S, and the singleton accounts for the number that has no partner inside the progression (here 5, since 124-5=119\notin the progression).  There are 9 two-element subsets and 1 singleton, for a total of 10 disjoint subsets.  Because |A|=11>10, the pigeonhole principle guarantees that at least one of the two-element subsets contains two members of A.  Those two members add up to S=124.  Hence A necessarily contains a pair of distinct integers whose sum is 124, as desired.

core_steps: Form disjoint subsets by pairing every term x of the progression with S−x, leaving at most two singletons
Compute the number of resulting subsets (pairs plus singletons)
Note that |A| is larger than that subset count
Invoke the pigeonhole principle to guarantee one full pair inside A
Conclude the two chosen numbers add up to the fixed sum S

slot1.description: size of the chosen set A

slot2.description: first term of the arithmetic progression

slot5.description: fixed sum S used for pairing

slot6.description: singleton produced by lack of partner in progression

slot7.description: singleton produced when x = S/2 lies in the progression
